symptom: Outbound emails fail with NDR 550 5.7.750 - Client blocked from sending from unregistered domain
rootCause: Sending domain not added as accepted domain in M365; or incorrectly configured connectors; or compromised account creating new inbound connectors
solution: Add and validate all sending domains in M365; use certificate-based outbound connector with accepted domain; check for unusual connectors and compromised accounts
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/Domain Restrictions (Un-provisioned Domains)(5.7.7.md

symptom: Inbound emails to non-existent recipients rejected with NDR 5.4.1 when DBEB is enabled
rootCause: Directory-Based Edge Blocking (DBEB) is configured to reject messages sent to invalid recipients at the edge
solution: Review DBEB configuration; ensure all valid recipients exist in Exchange Online directory; disable DBEB if causing issues with valid mail flow
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/Edge Restrictions (DBEB) (5.4.1).md

symptom: Emails rejected with NDR 4.7.500 Server busy or 5.7.708 with various AS codes (AS77712/AS77713/AS780082/AS780090/AS71721/AS63/AS66)
rootCause: Sending IP address throttled by EOP real-time reputation system due to poor reputation or lack of reputation (new IP hydration)
solution: Run Release Tenant IP Server Busy diagnostic in EOP Diagnostics to whitelist IP for up to 30 days; check IP spam percentage via EOP IP Health Diagnostic; for AS112201 codes escalate to engineering (diagnostic will not work)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/IP Restrictions or Throttling (Server Busy) (5.7.7.md

symptom: Outbound emails fail with NDR 550 5.1.8 Access denied bad outbound sender
rootCause: Sender account exceeded Exchange Online sending limits or account compromised and used for spam
solution: Check if account is compromised; if compromised follow Responding to a compromised email account guide; admin unblocks account on Restricted entities page in Microsoft 365 Defender portal (security.microsoft.com/restrictedusers); restrictions removed within 1 hour
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/Sender Restrictions and Limits (5.1.8, 5.1.90).md

symptom: Outbound emails fail with NDR 5.1.90 - reached daily limit for message recipients
rootCause: Sender exceeded daily recipient rate limit in Exchange Online; possible account compromise
solution: Verify account not compromised; wait for daily limit reset; review sending patterns against Exchange Online sending limits; refer to Troubleshooting Blocked Senders guide
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/Sender Restrictions and Limits (5.1.8, 5.1.90).md

symptom: All outbound emails from tenant blocked with NDR 550 5.7.705 Access denied tenant has exceeded threshold
rootCause: Tenant sending too much spam/bulk mail; common causes: compromised on-premises server routing to EXO, compromised admin account creating connectors, or expired subscription renewal
solution: 1) Check for fraud (do NOT run diagnostic if fraud); 2) Run Validate EOP Domain Health diagnostic; 3) Stop compromise: reset ALL admin passwords, enable MFA/Security Defaults; 4) Review outbound spam policy, connectors, mail flow; 5) After stopping compromise run recovery action to unblock; refer to aka.ms/list35
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/SAP_EOP NDR _ throttling Scope/Tenant Restrictions (Tenant Exceeded Threshold) (5.md

symptom: Emails rejected because sender IP address is listed on Spamhaus blocklist (CSS/SBL)
rootCause: Sender IP flagged by Spamhaus CSS for suspicious behavior (e.g. Anonymous tag in message header, spam-like sending patterns)
solution: Contact Spamhaus CSS team: 1) Go to https://contact-center.spamhaus.org/ select Other questions; 2) Provide IP list, business justification, request temporary whitelisting; 3) Email css-mmxvi@spamhaus.org for follow-up; PG contact: jdellapina@microsoft.com; expect 24hr response with case ID
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/Process to contact Spamhaus for blocked IP.md

symptom: Legitimate emails from China customers detected as malware due to URL scanning (e.g. cos.ap-beijing.myqcloud.com, inv-veri.chinatax.gov.cn, feishu.cn) - False Positive
rootCause: EOP URL scanning engine falsely flagged China-specific URLs (Tencent Cloud COS, official gov.cn, Feishu) as malicious via Sonar heuristic/cluster/ML/TI blocks
solution: File FP escalation via Assist 365 > Exchange Online Analysts (FPFN) with Network Message IDs; check Sonar AWB for existing tickets; reference ICM-528803532 for similar pattern; track in FN/FP pattern history for recurring pattern escalation
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/FN_FP Escalation/FN_FP tracking and template.md

symptom: Outbound emails rejected with NDR 550 5.0.350 Address rejected due to sender IP poor reputation
rootCause: Sender IP (e.g. 139.219.17.x China IPs) has poor reputation score in EOP/MDO reputation system
solution: File FP escalation via Assist 365 with sender IP and NDR details; request IP reputation review and whitelisting; Enterprise: EEE uses Get-ManualList to check/add to override list; Consumer (hotmail/live/outlook.com): whitelist IP in Surfer/KEStudio
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/FN_FP Escalation/FN_FP tracking and template.md

symptom: Legitimate emails blocked by domain blocklist (High confidence SPAM detection) - False Positive
rootCause: Sender domain incorrectly added to EOP domain blocklist causing high confidence spam verdict
solution: File FP escalation via Assist 365 > Exchange Online Analysts (FPFN); provide NMIDs and domain details; check Sonar AWB for domain block status; reference ICM-528358493 for similar pattern
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Process and Scope/FN_FP Escalation/FN_FP tracking and template.md

symptom: Slow mail delivery to EXO after batch resend from gateway - new sending IP gets deferred with 452 4.5.3 Too many recipients (AS780090) and 451 4.7.500 Access denied, emails delayed 54+ minutes
rootCause: New sending IP has no reputation in EOP; batch resending 25000+ emails triggers aggressive throttling; messages attributed to default inbound connector have much lower throughput limits than dedicated inbound connector
solution: PG adds new IP to allowlist for 1 month to build reputation via ICM; ensure messages are attributed to a dedicated Inbound Connector (check RECEIVE line in Extended Message Trace); run Release Tenant IP Server Busy diagnostic; avoid mass batch resend - use gradual sending
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/2512100040000821 Slow mail delivery to EXO.md

symptom: Sending IP blocked by Spamhaus due to DNS configuration issues - HELO greeting does not match sending IP, missing PTR record
rootCause: Mail server HELO domain A record does not resolve to the actual sending IP; missing reverse DNS (PTR) record; HELO/EHLO and PTR must be aligned for mail server identity verification
solution: Align HELO/EHLO hostname with DNS: 1) A record of HELO domain must point to sending IP; 2) PTR record of sending IP must resolve back to HELO domain; 3) Follow https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/dns-reverse-dns-for-azure-services for PTR setup; 4) After DNS fix, request Spamhaus delist via contact-center.spamhaus.org
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/2512100040000821 Slow mail delivery to EXO.md

symptom: 451 4.7.500 Access denied without AS code in response - back pressure message not from MDO antispam layer
rootCause: Transport layer back pressure (not MDO/antispam); no AS code behind the 4.7.500 means it originates from Exchange transport, not EOP filtering; typically caused by server load during high-volume sending
solution: If no AS code present, this is transport-layer back pressure, not MDO throttling - Release Tenant IP diagnostic will NOT help; sending server should implement retry logic (400-series = retry in same SMTP session); if persistent, escalate to Exchange transport engineering via ICM
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/2512100040000821 Slow mail delivery to EXO.md

symptom: Assist 365 Spam Verdict Reason diagnostic tool returns no results for 21v (Gallatin) EOP tenants
rootCause: Feature gap: Assist 365 diagnostic tool and tenant explorer do not support 21v Exchange Online tenants; Spam Verdict Reason tool is not available in Gallatin environment
solution: Use alternative diagnostic methods for 21v: 1) EXO PowerShell (Connect-ExchangeOnline -ExchangeEnvironmentName o365china); 2) Message trace in EAC portal (admin.exchange.microsoftonline.cn); 3) Message header analysis (mha.azurewebsites.net or mxtoolbox.com/EmailHeaders.aspx); 4) X-Forefront-Antispam-Report header for spam verdict
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/## Readiness/Readiness Content.md

symptom: Legitimate emails quarantined as CAT:SPOOF after passing through multi-hop relay chain (e.g. VW Group gateway -> on-premises Exchange -> 21V EOP) - SPF softfail + DKIM none -> DMARC fail
rootCause: Inbound Connector with EFSkipLastIP only traces back 1 hop; intermediate relay IPs (not in sender SPF) become the evaluated source IP; DKIM signature stripped by relay chain; Enhanced Filtering not configured to skip all intermediate IPs
solution: Configure Enhanced Filtering on Inbound Connector: Set-InboundConnector -EFSkipLastIP $false -EFSkipIPs <all-intermediate-IPs> -EFUsers <test-users-first>; verify X-MS-Exchange-ExternalOriginalInternetSender header appears; after testing remove -EFUsers to apply org-wide; keep rollback command ready
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP] Enhanced Filtering_Spoofed Senders Allow 解凳F.md

symptom: Intermittent SPF PermError (invalid SPF mechanism) after Enhanced Filtering configured - occurs sporadically for specific sender domains
rootCause: EOP DNS query timeout threshold is 500ms (1000ms extended); some sender domains DNS servers in APAC region respond in 1000-1600ms exceeding EOP timeout; EOP treats DNS timeout as SPF PermError which is by-design defensive behavior
solution: Add the spoofed sender to Spoof Intelligence allow list (external sender type) in SCC portal; this bypasses SPF check for known legitimate senders that consistently trigger DNS timeout
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP] Enhanced Filtering_Spoofed Senders Allow 解凳F.md

symptom: Auto-forwarded emails from Global M365 to 21V mailbox rejected with 550 5.7.509 - sending domain does not pass DMARC verification (DMARC policy=reject)
rootCause: SRS rewrites P1 From for SPF but does not fix DMARC alignment for forwarded messages; ARC (Authenticated Received Chain) which preserves original authentication results is NOT supported in 21V environment; DKIM signatures may break during forwarding
solution: Create Exchange Transport Rule (ETR) with SCL=-1 to bypass filtering for forwarded messages from trusted source; or add sending domain/IP to tenant allow list; ARC feature request filed (Issue 4949836) but no ETA for 21V
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP] automatically forwarded Emails rejected by D.md

symptom: Legitimate emails with URLs repeatedly flagged as malware (FP) - short-term whitelisting expires after 1 week to 1 month, causing recurring blocks
rootCause: EOP URL scanning detects certain URLs as malicious; standard FP whitelisting is temporary (1 week-1 month); Safe Links Trust/Allow feature not available in China 21V; no long-term URL allow mechanism available to support engineers
solution: Request Safe Links MustNotBrowse from Product Group for long-term URL whitelisting in China EOP; this is a PG-level action, not configurable by support engineer or customer directly
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP]How to Whitelist Emails with URLs for Long-Te.md

symptom: Outbound IPs repeatedly listed by Spamhaus daily despite delisting - multi-IP binding with one domain, delisting attempts frequently fail
rootCause: Macro-enabled Excel attachments (.xlsm) sent to large recipient lists trigger Spamhaus spamtrap detection; after initial reputation degradation, missing PTR records prevent successful delisting; Spamhaus multi-IP binding only recognizes top-listed IP; cumulative reputation damage makes re-listing increasingly difficult to recover from
solution: 1) Stop sending macro-enabled attachments externally (use compressed+password-protected delivery if unavoidable); 2) Configure PTR records for ALL outbound IPs; 3) Implement SPF/DKIM/DMARC; 4) Note: Spamhaus delisting requirements may not reflect original blocking reason - address root cause first; 5) Spamhaus does NOT support whitelisting
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP]Spamhaus IP Blocking.md

symptom: Emails from microsoft.com to 21V mailbox fail SPF check - SPF Fail for microsoft.com domain
rootCause: Email from microsoft.com routed through third-party relay (ttsenmta7.ttasia.com) before reaching 21V EOP; relay IP not in microsoft.com SPF record causing SPF fail; this is by-design behavior - SPF validates connecting IP against sender domain
solution: Recipient (21V tenant) must configure Enhanced Filtering for Connectors on Inbound Connector to allow EOP to trace back to original sending IP past the relay; PG confirmed this is by-design, sender-side fix not applicable
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/##Case Study/[EOP]microsoft.com 发送到21v 邮箱SPF Fail.md

symptom: User reported settings page visible in 21V SCC portal but backend infrastructure unavailable - settings cannot be saved or used
rootCause: 21V Gallatin environment lacks backend infrastructure for user reported settings feature; UI page not disabled despite backend unavailability (Bug 5529973); no ETA for fix as no funding allocated to disable the page
solution: Inform customer this is a known UI bug in 21V - the feature is not available; reference Bug 5529973; no workaround - user reported message submission not supported in 21V
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/Feature Gaps/Feature Bugs.md

symptom: Cannot access quarantine messages via API in 21V (Gallatin) - Quarantine API not available; MessageTrace API requires specific 21V endpoint and permissions
rootCause: Quarantine API not supported in 21V environment (pending PG - Issue 4866394); MessageTrace reporting API available but requires Exchange Administrator role on the app registration and 21V-specific endpoints
solution: Use MessageTrace Report API: 1) Register app in Azure AD, assign Exchange administrator role; 2) Get token: POST https://login.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantID}/oauth2/v2.0/token with scope https://partner.outlook.cn/.default; 3) GET https://partner.outlook.cn/ecp/reportingwebservice/reporting.svc/MessageTrace; 4) Filter by date: $filter=(StartDate eq datetime'YYYY-MM-DD') and (EndDate eq datetime'YYYY-MM-DD'); 5) Pagination: $top=2000&$skiptoken=2000; Quarantine API: track Issue 4866394
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/TSG & Tips/EOP API_ Quarantine & MessageTrace Report.md

symptom: Custom quarantine email notification not delivered to end users despite quarantine policy configuration with notification enabled
rootCause: Strict Preset Security Policy or Standard Preset Security Policy overrides custom quarantine policy settings; preset policies take higher priority and suppress custom quarantine notification configuration
solution: 1) Disable Strict/Standard Preset Security Policy in SCC portal (security.microsoftonline.cn); 2) Enable quarantine email notification in custom quarantine policy; 3) Apply custom quarantine policy in Anti-spam or Anti-phishing policy; 4) Test with GTUBE string: XJS*C4JDBQADN1.NSBN3*2IDNEN*GTUBE-STANDARD-ANTI-UBE-TEST-EMAIL*C.34X; 5) Notification frequency: every 4 hours / 1 day / 1 week depending on policy setting
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/TSG & Tips/How to configure quarantine email notification.md

symptom: Valid NDR (bounce message) detected as backscatter by EOP and delivered to recipient's junk folder instead of inbox
rootCause: EOP backscatter rule checks Message-ID/In-Reply-To headers against EXO format (<guid@*.CHNPR01.prod.partner.outlook.cn>); if Message-ID does not match EXO format, flagged as backscatter. Common triggers: 1) Email sent from iOS native email client (generates own Message-ID format); 2) Internal email in Hybrid Exchange deployment routed through on-premises Exchange first (on-prem generates non-EXO Message-ID format). Known issue: Task 4273311
solution: Request PG to add tenant to anti-backscatter rule allow list via ICM escalation; reference Known Issue Task 4273311 (Improve NDR BackScatter Fix to not discard valid emails and reject invalid emails); no customer-side workaround available
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/TSG & Tips/Known issue_ Valid NDR was detected as backscatter.md

symptom: Assist 365 SPAM Verdict Reason diagnostic tool and tenant explorer not available for 21v (Gallatin) EXO tenants - cannot use automated diagnostics to look up spam filter rule details
rootCause: 21v/Gallatin environment does not support Assist 365 diagnostic tools; tenant explorer is unavailable in sovereign cloud
solution: Manually inspect X-Forefront-Antispam-Report header from message headers to get SFS rule IDs; look up rule definitions in IPAntispamRule git repo (o365exchange.visualstudio.com/O365 Core/_git/IPAntispamRule); search processorID for detailed condition/action definitions
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 MDO EOP/Tools and Swarming/SPAM verdict reason analytics.md
